text: VO2peak from graded exercise tests with longer stage durations (3, 4, 7, 10 min) underestimates true VO2peak by approximately 1-5 mL/kg/min compared to a 1-min stage protocol.
Many traditional lactate threshold calculation methods do not provide valid estimates of maximal lactate steady state power.
A Modified Dmax method derived from a 4-minute stage GXT provides the most valid estimate of maximal lactate steady state, with a mean difference of only 1.1 W and ICC of 0.96.
VO2max and lactate threshold cannot be validly determined from a single graded exercise test, even with the inclusion of a verification exhaustive bout.
GXT protocol design variables such as stage duration significantly influence the determination of both VO2peak and lactate threshold values.
In trained male cyclists with VO2peak of ~61 mL/kg/min and peak power of ~420 W, the maximal lactate steady state occurs at approximately 264 W (~63% of peak power).
